contentBlocks.original:
विश्वम्भरी अखिल विश्व तनी जनेता
विद्या धरी वदनमा वसजो विधाता
दुर्बुद्धिने दूर करी सदबुद्धि आपो
माम पाहि ॐ भगवती भव दुःख कापो ॥ १ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
viśvambharī akhila viśva tanī janētā
vidyā dharī vadanamā vasajō vidhātā
durbuddhinē dūra karī sadabuddhi āpō
māma pāhi ōṃ bhagavatī bhava duḥkha kāpō ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Viśvambharī — sustainer of the entire universe, creator of all beings, bearer of wisdom — O creator seated as knowledge on the face of Brahmā — drive away foolish understanding and grant good understanding. O mother, protect [us].
contentBlocks.meanings: viśvambharī: sustainer of all the worlds | akhila-viśva-tanī: sustainer of the entire universe | janetā: creator | vidyā-dharī: bearer of wisdom/knowledge | vadanamā vasajo vidhātā: seated as knowledge on the face of Brahmā | durbuddhi: foolish/wicked understanding | dūr karī: drive away | sadabuddhi: good understanding | āpo: grant | mā: O mother | rakṣo: protect

contentBlocks.original:
भूलो पडयी भवरने भटकू भवानी
सूझे नहीं लगिर कोई दिशा जवानी
भासे भयङ्कर वाली मन ना उतापो
माम पाहि ॐ भगवती भव दुःख कापो ॥ २ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhūlō paḍayī bhavaranē bhaṭakū bhavānī
sūjhē nahīṃ lagira kōī diśā javānī
bhāsē bhayaṅkara vālī mana nā utāpō
māma pāhi ōṃ bhagavatī bhava duḥkha kāpō ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Bhavānī — I have fallen into the whirlpool of worldly existence and am wandering lost. I can see no direction at this age. The frightening veil of māyā covers the mind's fire. O Jananī (mother), seeing my pain, take me across.
contentBlocks.meanings: bhavānī: the goddess of existence | bhūlo paḍayī: having fallen (gone astray) | bhavarne bhaṭakū: wandering in the whirlpool of existence | sūjhe nahīṃ: I cannot see/discern | lagīr koī diśā: any direction | javānī: of this age | bhāse bhayaṅkara vālī: the frightening [māyā] covers | man nā utāpo: the fire of the mind | jananī: mother

contentBlocks.original:
आ रङ्कने उगरावा नथी कोई आरो
जन्माण्ड छू जननी हु ग्रही बाल तारो
ना शु सुनो भगवती शिशु ना विलापो
माम पाहि ॐ भगवती भव दुःख कापो ॥ ३ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ā raṅkanē ugarāvā nathī kōī ārō
janmāṇḍa Chū jananī hu grahī bāla tārō
nā śu sunō bhagavatī śiśu nā vilāpō
māma pāhi ōṃ bhagavatī bhava duḥkha kāpō ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: There is no one to uplift this destitute one — O Jananī, I have taken birth from you; I am your child. Will you not hear, O Bhagavatī, the weeping of your child? O mother, you who are the giver of milk, do not let go of my hand.
contentBlocks.meanings: ā raṅkane ugarāvā: to uplift this destitute one | nathī koī āro: there is no support/shelter | janmāṇḍ: having been born | jananī: O mother | hū: I | grahī bāl tāro: taking me as your child | nā śu: will you not | suno: hear | bhagavatī: O Bhagavatī (the divine one) | śiśu: child | vegaṇo: cry/weeping | mā: O mother | dūdhnī: who gives milk (nursing mother) | hāth: hand | na mūko: do not let go

contentBlocks.original:
मा कर्म जन्मा कथनी करता विचारू
आ स्रुष्टिमा तुज विना नथी कोई मारू
कोने कहू कथन योग तनो बलापो
माम पाहि ॐ भगवती भव दुःख कापो ॥ ४ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mā karma janmā kathanī karatā vichārū
ā sruṣṭimā tuja vinā nathī kōī mārū
kōnē kahū kathana yōga tanō balāpō
māma pāhi ōṃ bhagavatī bhava duḥkha kāpō ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: I think about what my karma and birth should say — in this creation, without you there is no one who is mine. To whom shall I tell the story of my yoga's path? O mother, only you know my inner secrets.
contentBlocks.meanings: mā karma-janmā: my karma and birth | kathanī: what should be said | kartā vicāro: I think | ā sṛṣṭimā: in this creation | tuj vinā: without you | nathī koī māro: there is no one who is mine | kone kahū: to whom shall I tell | kathana yoga tano: the story of yoga's path | māro: my | antarno: inner | antaryāmī: the knower of inner secrets

contentBlocks.original:
हूवो काम क्रोध मद मोह थकी छकेलो
आदम्बरे अति घनो मदथी बकेलो
दोषों थकी दूषित ना करी माफ़ पापो
माम पाहि ॐ भगवती भव दुःख कापो ॥ ५ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
hūvō kāma krōdha mada mōha thakī Chakēlō
ādambarē ati ghanō madathī bakēlō
dōṣōṃ thakī dūṣita nā karī māpha़ pāpō
māma pāhi ōṃ bhagavatī bhava duḥkha kāpō ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: I became intoxicated by kāma, krodha (anger), mad (pride), and moha (delusion) — was extremely intoxicated with the noise of empty display. Faults having made me impure — do not cause [me to] be forgiven without purifying [me]. O mother, I am your servant.
contentBlocks.meanings: kāma-krodha-mad-moha: desire, anger, pride, and delusion | thakī chakeḷo: became intoxicated | ādambhare: with empty display/ostentation | ati ghano: exceedingly | madathī bakeḷo: intoxicated with pride | doṣoṃ thakī dūṣit: made impure by faults | nā karī māph: do not let me be forgiven [without purification] | sevak: servant

contentBlocks.original:
ना शाश्त्रना श्रवण नु पयपान किधू
ना मन्त्र के स्तुति कथा नथी काई किधू
श्रद्धा धरी नथी करा तव नाम जापो
माम पाहि ॐ भगवती भव दुःख कापो ॥ ६ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nā śāśtranā śravaṇa nu payapāna kidhū
nā mantra kē stuti kathā nathī kāī kidhū
śraddhā dharī nathī karā tava nāma jāpō
māma pāhi ōṃ bhagavatī bhava duḥkha kāpō ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: I have not drunk the milk of hearing the scriptures. I have done nothing — no mantra, no prayer, no story. I have not kept faith and have not surrendered. O mother, accept the one who has come with only devotion.
contentBlocks.meanings: śāstranā śravaṇ nu payapān kidhū: not drunk the milk of scripture-hearing | mantra ke stuti kathā nathī kāī kidhū: done nothing — no mantra, prayer, or story | śraddhā dharī nathī: have not kept faith | kartā: I doing | mā: O mother | kevaḷ bhakti sāthe: with only devotion | āveḷā: the one who has come | svikar: accept

contentBlocks.original:
रे रे भवानी बहु भूल थी छे मारी
आ जवोइन्दगी थी मने अतिशे अकारि
दोषों प्रजाली सगला तवा छाप छापो
माम पाहि ॐ भगवती भव दुःख कापो ॥ ७ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
rē rē bhavānī bahu bhūla thī Chē mārī
ā javōindagī thī manē atiśē akāri
dōṣōṃ prajālī sagalā tavā Chāpa Chāpō
māma pāhi ōṃ bhagavatī bhava duḥkha kāpō ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Bhavānī — many, many are my mistakes. This life has become greatly burdensome to me. Having burned all faults — O great lamp of your [light] — see: in this whirlpool I am drowning.
contentBlocks.meanings: re re bhavānī: O O Bhavānī (emotional exclamation) | bahu bhūl thī che mārī: many are my mistakes | ā javoinadagī: this wretched life | mane atiśe akāri: has become greatly burdensome/unbearable to me | doṣoṃ prajāḷī sagaḷā: having burned all faults | tavā chāpa: your stamp/mark | mahā dīpamā: O great lamp | jo: see | ā bhavarāmā: in this whirlpool | ḍūbaū: I am drowning

contentBlocks.original:
खाली न कोई स्थल छे विण आप धारो
ब्रह्माण्डमा अणु अणु महि वास तारो
शक्तिन माप गणवा अगणीत मापों
माम पाहि ॐ भगवती भव दुःख कापो ॥ ८ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
khālī na kōī sthala Chē viṇa āpa dhārō
brahmāṇḍamā aṇu aṇu mahi vāsa tārō
śaktina māpa gaṇavā agaṇīta māpōṃ
māma pāhi ōṃ bhagavatī bhava duḥkha kāpō ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: There is no place empty of your presence — in every atom (aṇu) of the cosmos your dwelling (vāsa) is. To measure the extent of [your] power is beyond counting, O mother — in the sky, in the water, in the earth, you dwell.
contentBlocks.meanings: khāḷī na koī sthal che viṇ āp dhāro: there is no place empty of your presence | brahmāṇḍamā: in the cosmos | aṇu aṇu mahī: in every atom | vāsa tāro: your dwelling | śaktina māp gaṇavā: to measure the extent of power | agaṇīt: beyond counting | māmā: O mother | ākāśe: in the sky | jale: in the water | bhūmī: in the earth | tame rahṛo: you dwell

contentBlocks.original:
पापे प्रपञ्च करवा बधी वाते पुरो
खोटो खरो भगवती पण हूँ तमारो
जद्यान्धकार दूर सदबुध्ही आपो
माम पाहि ॐ भगवती भव दुःख कापो ॥ ९ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
pāpē prapañcha karavā badhī vātē purō
khōṭō kharō bhagavatī paṇa hūँ tamārō
jadyāndhakāra dūra sadabudhhī āpō
māma pāhi ōṃ bhagavatī bhava duḥkha kāpō ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: I am ready in all ways to commit more sins — O Bhagavatī, wrong or right, I am yours. Remove the darkness of ignorance, grant good wisdom, and look — I am here, your servant, at your feet.
contentBlocks.meanings: pāpe prapañca karavā: to commit sinful activities | badhī vāte puro: ready in all ways | khoṭo kharo: wrong or right | bhagavatī: O Bhagavatī | paṇ hūṃ tamāro: but I am yours | jadyā-andhakār: the darkness of ignorance | dūr: remove | sadabuddhī āpo: grant good wisdom | sevak: servant | caraṇe: at the feet

contentBlocks.original:
शीखे सुने रसिक चन्दज एक चित्ते
तेना थकी विविधः ताप तलेक चिते
वाधे विशेष वली अम्बा तना प्रतापो
माम पाहि ॐ भगवती भव दुःख कापो ॥ १० ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śīkhē sunē rasika chandaja ēka chittē
tēnā thakī vividhaḥ tāpa taḻēka chitē
vādhē viśēṣa valī ambā tanā pratāpō
māma pāhi ōṃ bhagavatī bhava duḥkha kāpō ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Rasika Canda — listening and learning with one mind — from that [practice], various types of afflictions (tāpa) are extinguished in the heart. Mother Ambā's name grows and spreads — through that, liberation is attained, O mind, recite!
contentBlocks.meanings: śīkhe sune: learning and listening | rasika canda: the poet-saint Rasika Canda | eka citte: with one mind/single-pointedly | tena thakī: from that | vividha tāp: various types of affliction | taleṭa cinte: extinguished in the heart | vādhe viśeṣa: grows specially | ambā tanā: of mother Ambā | nāme: in the name | mukti: liberation | ṭhame: is attained/steadied | manḍa: O mind | bhaje: recite/worship

contentBlocks.original:
श्री सदगुरु शरणमा रहीने भजु छू
रात्री दिने भगवती तुजने भजु छू
सदभक्त सेवक तना परिताप छापो
माम पाहि ॐ भगवती भव दुःख कापो ॥ ११ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrī sadaguru śaraṇamā rahīnē bhaju Chū
rātrī dinē bhagavatī tujanē bhaju Chū
sadabhakta sēvaka tanā paritāpa Chāpō
māma pāhi ōṃ bhagavatī bhava duḥkha kāpō ॥ 11 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Remaining in the shelter of the true Guru, I worship — day and night, O Bhagavatī, I worship you. The sorrows of the true devotee-servant are extinguished — O mother, having come to your feet, my mind has found rest.
contentBlocks.meanings: śrī sadguru śaraṇamā rahīne: remaining in the shelter of the true Guru | bhaju chū: I worship | rātrī dine: night and day | bhagavatī tujne: O Bhagavatī, you | sadabhakta-sevak: true devotee-servant | paritāpa: sorrows/afflictions | chāpa: extinguished | mā: O mother | caraṇe āvī: having come to the feet | mano: my mind | samāpyo: has found rest/completion

contentBlocks.original:
अन्तर विशे अधिक उर्मी तता भवानी
ग्AUवो स्तुति तव बले नमिने मृगानी
संसारना सकल रोग समूल कापो
माम पाहि ॐ भगवती भव दुःख कापो ॥ १२ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
antara viśē adhika urmī tatā bhavānī
gāūvō stuti tava balē naminē mṛgānī
saṃsāranā sakaḻa rōga samūḻa kāpō
māma pāhi ōṃ bhagavatī bhava duḥkha kāpō ॥ 12 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: In the heart, the inner longing grows, O Bhavānī — through the force of praising [you], I bow and sing, O Mṛgāṇī (the doe-eyed goddess). May all the ailments of worldly existence be completely uprooted — may the mother's name resound in every heart.
contentBlocks.meanings: antarviśe adhik urmī tatā: greater longing arising inwardly | bhavānī: O Bhavānī | gauvā stuti: to sing praises | tava bale: through the force/power of your [name] | namīne: bowing | mṛgāṇī: the doe-eyed goddess (Mṛgāṇī, an epithet of the Goddess) | saṃsāranā sakal rog: all ailments of worldly existence | samūl: completely/by the roots | haro: may they be removed | mānā nāme: in the mother's name
